questions.question: What is the WCAG 2.2 AA minimum contrast ratio for normal body text against its background?
questions.explanation: AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text and non-text UI elements. The 7:1 bar is AAA, which is stricter than what a typical blog targets.

questions.question: You're implementing a dark-mode toggle. A user with system dark mode enabled who has never clicked the toggle visits the site. What should happen?
choices: Show light mode - manual control is more predictable.
Show dark mode, because the `prefers-color-scheme: dark` media query applies and no `data-theme` override is set.
Show dark mode, but only after the JavaScript runs and sets `data-theme="dark"`.
Show whichever mode was last used by any visitor.
questions.explanation: If `localStorage` has no saved choice, the script should not set `data-theme` at all - letting the `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` CSS rule apply naturally. This honours the system preference and avoids any flash because no JavaScript is needed to render the correct mode.

questions.question: A reset CSS in your theme includes `*:focus { outline: none; }`. A designer argues the default focus ring is ugly and should stay removed. What's the right response?
choices: Agree - visual polish matters more than focus rings for a blog.
Keep `outline: none` but add a hover effect; that covers most users.
Remove the rule and restyle focus with `:focus-visible` - never delete keyboard focus indicators.
Only show focus rings to users with a screen reader enabled.
questions.explanation: Removing focus indicators breaks the page for keyboard users, who have no other signal for where focus is. The right trade-off is to keep them visible but restyle to match the design, and use `:focus-visible` so they appear only for keyboard navigation, not mouse clicks.
